key_findings: ALL 10 agents completed successfully (0 failures) — up from ~50% in iteration 1
Baseline NEVER evaluates all 8 styles (0/5) — consistently covers only 3-5
Baseline rarely uses cost as systematic decision factor (2/5)
Baseline produces inconsistent architecture.md structure (1/5 with full format)
Both make reasonable architecture recommendations — skill advantage is rigor and structure
non_discriminating_assertions: A0 (agent completion): 10/10 — all agents succeeded in iteration 2
A1 (correct recommendation): Both variants make defensible choices
skill_advantages: Systematic evaluation of ALL 8 styles every time
Handling unmapped characteristics via mapping table
Standardized architecture.md output (rationale, tradeoffs, evolution path)
Red flag detection (premature distribution, resume-driven architecture)
Qualifying context questions when scores are close
